😉This strategy is only for US30 and XAUUSD.

First you make a 15 minute ORB in Tokyo session. Then go to 5 minute chart and look for a strong POI around it. When both ORB and POI break and retest, then only take the trade. Every trade has to be closed at 1:2 ratio. If you maintain 5% daily then the capital will double at the end of the month, and you will withdraw only 10%, the rest will be compounded again for the next month.

Important Note

This strategy is tested and used only on US30 & XAUUSD.
If you want to apply it to any other market:

First select the market.

Apply the same strategy.

Do a 2–3 month backtest.

If results are consistent, then you can trade that market using this strategy.
Strategy (Tokyo Session Trading Plan)

1. Trade only during the Tokyo Session.

2. First, mark the 15-minute Opening Range (ORB) → that means marking the High & Low of the first 15 minutes after the session opens.

3. Now shift your chart to the 5-minute timeframe.

4. Look for a POI (Point of Interest) near the ORB High/Low.
-----/-----

🔎 Types of POI (Point of Interest)

1. FVG (Fair Value Gap)
2. OB (Order Block)
3. Support & Resistance
4. Trend Line
-----/-----

⚡ Entry Rules

When both the ORB (15 min high/low) and the POI (any of the 4 types above) are broken →
→ then wait for a retest.

Enter the trade after retest.

Target = 1:2 Risk to Reward Ratio (RR)
-----/----

💰 Profit Target Rules

Daily goal = 5% profit of your capital.

Monthly target = 100% capital growth.

After 6 month withdraw every month  only 10% of your capital, keep the rest for compounding. 

⚡Important Note

This strategy is tested and used only on US30 & XAUUSD. 

If you want to apply it to any other market:

First select the market.

Apply the same strategy.

Do a 1-2 month backtest.

If results are consistent, then you can trade that market using this strategy.

If that would work, 90% of traders would make money. Actually, 90% of traders lose money, and the ORB isn´t working any better than a coin toss.

One of the biggest traps newer traders fall into—assuming volume on short timeframes in index futures like NQ and ES is clean or predictive. It’s not. It’s fragmented, delayed, and often misleading. 
Fragmented Liquidity: Volume is spread across multiple exchanges and dark pools. What you see on your DOM or chart is just a slice.
Delayed Prints: Some platforms batch or delay volume updates, especially on volatile opens. You’re reacting to stale data.
Spoofing & Absorption: High-frequency players manipulate visible volume. What looks like a breakout might be engineered bait.

Using 5 minutes or 15 minutes orb can be your biggest mistake. Low Signal-to-Noise Ratio: Especially in the first 5 minutes, price action is often erratic. You're trading noise, not conviction. ORBs can lure you into weak moves that reverse hard.
Overtrading Risk: ORB setups tempt you to act fast, often before trend confirmation. That leads to impulsive trades and emotional fatigue.
Lack of Edge Without Filters: Traders who succeed with ORB often layer in filters—VWAP confluence, higher timeframe trend, volume surge, or macro alignment. Without those, you're just flipping coins.
Market Regime Blindness: ORB works better in trending environments. In chop or news-driven whipsaws, it’s a recipe for getting chopped up.
Use ORB as a Reference, Not a Trigger: Treat the range as a zone of interest. Look for retests, volume confirmation, or higher timeframe alignment before entering.
Pair with Macro or Event-Driven Bias: If you're trading futures, align ORB setups with FOMC, CPI, or earnings catalysts. That gives the breakout teeth.
Trade the Reaction, Not the Initial Move: Often the second push after a failed ORB gives better risk/reward.
90% of 5-min ORBs are untradeable unless they align with higher timeframe trends.
